name: Raja Koduri
currentRole: Former Senior Vice President, Accelerated Computing Systems and Graphics Group
formerRole: Chief Architect and Senior Vice President at Intel
connectionToCompany: Led Intel's Xe GPU program directly competing with NVIDIA's GeForce and data center GPU lines; previously worked at AMD competing with NVIDIA before joining Intel
sourceNote: Widely covered in industry press as head of Intel's GPU and accelerated computing division; departed Intel in 2023

name: Lisa Pearce
connectionToCompany: Leads Intel graphics software stack development, directly competing with NVIDIA's driver and software ecosystem including CUDA-adjacent graphics APIs
sourceNote: Referenced in Intel graphics engineering and open-source driver community documentation

name: Avinash Sodani
currentRole: Vice President, Data Center and AI Group
formerRole: Lead Architect on Intel Knights Landing (Xeon Phi)
connectionToCompany: Leads Intel's Gaudi AI accelerator line which competes directly with NVIDIA's H100 and A100 data center GPUs for AI training and inference workloads
sourceNote: Identified in Intel press releases and HPC industry coverage related to Gaudi and data center AI products

name: Wei Li
currentRole: Vice President and General Manager, AI and Analytics Division
formerRole: Director of Machine Learning at Intel
connectionToCompany: Oversees Intel's AI software stack including OpenVINO and oneAPI which compete with NVIDIA's CUDA ecosystem for developer mindshare in AI and ML workloads
sourceNote: Referenced in Intel AI developer documentation, conference presentations, and press materials related to oneAPI and OpenVINO

name: Trish Damkroger
currentRole: Vice President and General Manager, High Performance Computing
formerRole: Associate Director for Computation at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
connectionToCompany: Led Intel's HPC business unit competing with NVIDIA's dominant position in supercomputing accelerators; relevant to understanding competitive dynamics in the TOP500 supercomputer market
sourceNote: Identified in Intel HPC press releases, SC supercomputing conference coverage, and industry analyst reports
